tasks.title: Split parallel run command into focused modules
evidence: find crates/ralph/src -name '*.rs' ! -path '*/tests/*' ! -name '*tests.rs' -print0 | xargs -0 wc -l | sort -nr | head -n 15
crates/ralph/src/commands/run/parallel/mod.rs: 1421 lines (wc -l).
AGENTS.md: files should stay ~400 LOC; >700 LOC requires explicit justification.
plan: Identify cohesive responsibilities within the module and define new submodule boundaries.
Extract grouped functionality into new submodules with clear interfaces and minimal pub surface.
Update imports/exports and module docs so callers use the new structure.
Adjust or add tests around the extracted units to cover success and failure modes.
Verify by running `make ci` and confirm the file(s) are <= 700 LOC.
notes: Principles: A (KISS), D (Localize change / SRP).
Why it matters: oversized modules make changes risky, increase merge conflicts, and obscure invariants.
Minimal fix: split into cohesive submodules without expanding public API.
Acceptance: new modules keep responsibilities focused, tests pass, and no file exceeds ~700 LOC without justification.
